cli.description: Manage machines, repositories, and deployments over SSH

warnings.licenseSignatureRefreshed: Refreshed {{count}} repo license(s) with invalid signatures after renet update
warnings.licenseSignatureRefreshFailed: {{count}} repo license(s) have invalid signatures after renet update. Run: rdc subscription refresh -m <machine>

agent.descriptionShort: Agent-friendly utilities for AI coding assistants
agent.description: Introspection commands for AI agents. Key subcommands: capabilities (list all commands with args/options as JSON), schema (detailed schema for a single command), exec (execute a command with JSON input from stdin, always returns JSON).

capabilities.description: List all available commands with arguments and options

schema.description: Show detailed schema for a specific command

exec.description: Execute a command with JSON input from stdin

generateReference.description: Generate command reference markdown for Claude skills

mcp.descriptionShort: Model Context Protocol server for AI agents
mcp.description: Expose the CLI as a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for integration with AI agents. The server wraps CLI commands as MCP tools with structured JSON input/output. Key subcommand: serve (start stdio-based MCP server).

serve.configOption: Config name to use for all commands
serve.allowGrandOption: Allow destructive operations on grand (non-fork) repositories. Default: fork-only mode

repo_fork.parentDescription: Parent repository name (or name:tag) to fork from
repo_fork.tagDescription: Tag for the fork (creates name:tag, e.g. "staging")

status.notAuthenticated: Not authenticated. Run: rdc auth login

register.description: Register a new organization and user account
register.success: Registration successful! Check your email for the activation code.

activate.success: Account activated! You can now login with: rdc auth login

disable.confirm: Are you sure you want to disable TFA?

enable.addToApp: Add this to your authenticator app

fork.description: Create a forked token for another application

trace.description: Trace audit history for an entity

machines.description: List machines in a Ceph cluster

create.description: Create a new RBD clone from snapshot

machines.description: List machines assigned to a clone

doctor.descriptionShort: Diagnose CLI environment and renet integration
doctor.description: Run diagnostic checks on the CLI environment: Node.js version, renet binary availability, config file validity, SSH key status, and network connectivity. Outputs a structured health report with pass/fail indicators.
doctor.title: Rediacc CLI Doctor

config.descriptionShort: Manage config files, machines, and SSH connections
config.description: Manage CLI configuration files stored at ~/.config/rediacc/. CRUD operations for machines, repositories, storage backends, and SSH keys within a config. Key subcommands: init (create named config), show, list, set (defaults), recover (from backup). Nested groups: config machine, config repository, config storage, config infra. To sync config across devices, use `store`.

init.description: Create a new named config file
init.optionMasterPassword: Encrypt resources with a master password
init.s3Required: --s3-endpoint, --s3-bucket, and --s3-access-key-id are required for S3 resource state
init.s3SecretRequired: S3 secret access key is required

list.noConfigs: No config files found. Create one with: rdc config init

show.noConfig: No active config. Create one with: rdc config init

set.description: Set a default value (team, region, bridge, machine)

recover.failed: Failed to restore config "{{name}}" from backup

setSsh.description: Update SSH configuration for the current config

setRenet.description: Set the path to renet binary

machine.description: Manage machines in the current config

add.description: Add a machine to the current config. Auto-scans SSH host keys. After adding, run: config machine setup <name>
add.descriptionShort: Add a machine to the current config

remove.description: Remove a machine from the current config

list.description: List machines in the current config
list.noMachines: No machines configured. Add one with: rdc config machine add <name> --ip <ip> --user <user>

scanKeys.description: Scan SSH host keys for machines in the current config

setup.description: Provision a remote machine for repositories (installs renet, configures Docker, BTRFS datastore). Idempotent. Required after 'config machine add' and before 'repo create'
setup.descriptionShort: Provision a remote machine for repositories

setCeph.description: Set Ceph RBD configuration for a machine

repository.description: Manage repository mappings in the current config

add.description: Add a repository GUID mapping to the current config
add.optionGuid: Repository GUID (UUID from storage backup filenames)
add.optionNetworkId: Network ID for Docker isolation (2816, 2880, ...). Auto-assigned if omitted

remove.description: Remove a repository mapping from the current config

list.description: List repository GUID mappings in the current config
list.noRepositories: No repositories configured. Add one with: rdc config repository add <name> --guid <guid>

add.optionToken: API token for the cloud provider

add.optionIpv6Output: Custom: output attribute for IPv6 address

import.description: Import storages from an rclone config file

set.description: Set infrastructure configuration for a machine (machine-specific: IPs, domain, ports; shared: cert email, CF DNS token)
set.descriptionShort: Set infrastructure configuration for a machine

set.optionCfDnsToken: Cloudflare DNS API token for ACME DNS-01 challenge (shared across machines)

store.descriptionShort: Manage config sync stores (S3, local file, Bitwarden, Git, Vault)
store.description: Sync CLI config files to external backends for multi-device access. Supported backends: S3, local file, Bitwarden, Git, HashiCorp Vault. Key subcommands: add (register store), list, remove, push (upload config), pull (download config), sync (bidirectional with version awareness). Not to be confused with `storage` which manages backup storage providers.

add.optionType: Store type (s3, local-file, bitwarden, git, vault)
add.optionBwFolderId: Bitwarden folder ID to organize config items (optional)
add.s3Required: --s3-endpoint, --s3-bucket, and --s3-access-key-id are required for S3 stores
add.s3SecretRequired: S3 secret access key is required
add.optionVaultMount: Vault KV v2 mount path (default: secret)
add.optionVaultPrefix: Path prefix within Vault KV engine (default: rdc/configs)
add.vaultTokenRequired: Vault token is required (pass --vault-token or set VAULT_TOKEN env)
add.addedButUnverified: Store "{{name}}" added but connection could not be verified

list.noStores: No stores configured. Add one with: rdc store add <name> --type <type>

sync.description: Sync config with stores (pull then push)

machine.descriptionShort: Manage remote machines (query status, list, create, delete)
machine.description: Inspect remote machine state: system resources, running containers, systemd services, deployed repositories, and health diagnostics. Read-only queries — to modify repos use `repo`. Key subcommands: query (full status with --system, --containers, --repositories, --services filters), list, create, rename, delete, prune.

delete.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete machine "{{name}}"?

vault-status.description: Show parsed vault status for a machine (cloud adapter only)

health.description: Check machine health for CI/CD pipelines
health.noData: No status data available for machine

containers.description: List containers on a machine with status, health, and domain routing. JSON includes full container details with repository resolved to name (original in repository_guid), domain, and autoRoute
containers.noContainers: No containers found on this machine
containers.healthCheck: Health check mode - exits with code 2 if any unhealthy

services.description: List rediacc-managed systemd services on a machine (name, state, sub-state, restart count, memory, repository resolved to name with original in repository_guid). Use --stability-check to exit with code 2 if any failed or restarting (for CI/CD)
services.descriptionShort: List rediacc-managed systemd services on a machine
services.noServices: No rediacc services found on this machine
services.stabilityCheck: Stability check mode - exits with code 2 if any failed/restarting

query.description: Show full machine status (infra, system, repos with name/guid, containers with repository/repository_guid/domain/autoRoute, services with repository/repository_guid)

vaultStatus.description: Show parsed vault status for a machine

repos.description: List deployed repositories on a machine (name, GUID, size, mount status, Docker state, container count, disk usage, modified date, Rediaccfile present). JSON nests containers and services under each repo. Use --search to filter by name or GUID
repos.descriptionShort: List deployed repositories on a machine
repos.noRepos: No repositories deployed on this machine

testConnection.description: Test SSH connection to a machine and capture host key

provision.optionSshUser: SSH username for the new VM (default: root)
provision.optionInfra: Also configure infrastructure (infra set + infra push)
provision.optionBaseDomain: Base domain for infrastructure (e.g., example.com). Implies --infra
provision.infraInherited: Auto-detected base domain from sibling machine "{{source}}"
provision.noInfraHint: No base domain configured. Run 'rdc config infra set <machine> --base-domain <domain>' then 'rdc config infra push <machine>' to enable DNS and proxy.

prune.description: Remove orphaned datastore resources and stale snapshots from a machine
prune.descriptionShort: Prune orphaned resources and stale snapshots
prune.dryRunOption: Show what would be removed without making changes
prune.startingDatastore: Scanning for orphaned datastore resources on {{machine}}...
prune.orphanedReposOption: Also prune repo images not in any config

maintenance.description: Enable or disable maintenance mode (blocks non-admin logins)

ops.description: Local VM provisioning and management using KVM/QEMU/Hyper-V (platform auto-detected). Not for remote machines — use `machine` for those. Key subcommands: up (start VMs), down (stop), status (list running VMs), ssh (connect to VM), setup (install prerequisites), check (verify host readiness).

ssh.vmNotFound: VM with ID {{id}} not found or not running

datastore.descriptionShort: Manage Ceph RBD datastore lifecycle (init, fork, unfork, status)
datastore.description: Low-level block storage management for machine datastores (Ceph RBD or local BTRFS). Typically only needed during initial machine setup or cross-machine migration. Key subcommands: init (create datastore), status (health/usage), fork (clone datastore to another machine), unfork (restore from snapshot).
datastore.machineOption: Machine name (where the Ceph datastore is hosted)

init.description: Initialize datastore on a machine (local loop-backed or Ceph RBD)
init.poolOption: Ceph pool name (default: from machine config, or rbd)
init.imageOption: Ceph RBD image name (default: from machine config via set-ceph)
init.forceOption: Force initialization even if datastore already exists (WARNING: reformats storage)
init.imageRequired: --image is required for ceph backend (or set via: rdc config set-ceph -m <machine> --image <name>)

fork.description: Create a local COW copy of a Ceph datastore via RBD snapshot + clone (< 2s). Save snapshot/clone names from output for unfork. Only one fork per target name; unfork before re-forking
fork.descriptionShort: Create a local COW copy of a Ceph datastore
fork.toOption: Clone suffix (creates clone named <image>-fork-<name>). The fork stays on the source machine
fork.cowSizeOption: COW backing file size (default: auto, grows on demand)

unfork.description: Clean up a fork: unmount COW, remove clone, remove snapshot
unfork.sourceOption: Original RBD image that was forked (from config set-ceph --image, e.g. ds-prod)
unfork.snapshotOption: Snapshot to remove (the "Snapshot:" value from fork output, e.g. fork-<timestamp>)
unfork.destOption: Clone image to remove (the "Clone:" value from fork output, e.g. ds-prod-fork-<machine>)
unfork.poolOption: Ceph pool name (default: from machine config)
unfork.forceOption: Continue cleanup even if individual steps fail (e.g. unmount, snapshot removal)

status.description: Show datastore backend, size, usage, mount status, and cow_mode (if forked)

register.description: Register the protocol handler on the system
register.adminRequired: Note: This may require administrator/root privileges.
register.currentUser: Registering protocol handler for current user...
register.exampleUrls: You can now open URLs like:

unregister.description: Unregister the protocol handler from the system

status.title: Protocol Handler Status
status.registerHint: To register the protocol handler, run:

open.description: Open a {{scheme}}:// URL and execute the action

parse.description: Parse a {{scheme}}:// URL and show components

add.description: Add a permission to a group

remove.description: Remove a permission from a group

delete.confirm: Are you sure you want to delete task {{taskId}}?

delete.grandRepoWarning: This is a grand repository (credential). Deleting it is irreversible.
delete.confirm: Delete repository "{{name}}"? This cannot be undone.

promote.description: Promote a fork to grand status
promote.separateInfo: This will separate "{{name}}" from grand repository "{{grandName}}"
promote.siblingsInfo: {{count}} sibling clone(s) will remain linked to the original grand:

repo.description: Deploy and manage encrypted repositories on machines. Key subcommands: create, up (deploy), down (stop), fork (instant CoW clone), delete, push/pull (backup), sync (file transfer). Lifecycle: repo create → repo up --mount → repo down. See Key Concepts in rdc --help for architecture, routing, and environment variable details.
repo.descriptionShort: Deploy and manage encrypted repositories on machines
repo.noCredential: Warning: Repository "{{name}}" has no credential set. Operations on encrypted repos may fail.

mount.description: Mount a repository (decrypt and open the LUKS container, making the filesystem accessible). Needed on first deploy, after 'repo push' to a new machine, or after 'repo unmount'. Can also be done via 'repo up --mount'. The volume stays mounted until explicitly unmounted. Omit name to mount all repos on the machine
mount.descriptionShort: Mount a repository (decrypt and open LUKS container)
mount.checkpointOption: Restore CRIU container checkpoint after mount (resume processes from saved memory state). Used after 'repo push --checkpoint' for live migration
mount.noDockerOption: Skip starting Docker daemon after mount

unmount.description: Unmount a repository (close the LUKS container, detaching the encrypted filesystem). Services must be stopped first ('repo down'). After unmount, repo data is inaccessible until remounted. Required before 'repo resize'. Omit name to unmount all repos on the machine
unmount.checkpointOption: Create CRIU container checkpoint before unmount (capture running process memory state for later restore)

up.description: Deploy or update a repository (mount, run Rediaccfile up which calls renet compose). Proxy routes take ~3s to become active after deploy. Use --mount for first deploy or forked repos. CRIU checkpoint restore is auto-detected — use --skip-checkpoint to force fresh start. Omit name to deploy all repos on the machine
up.descriptionShort: Deploy or update a repository on a machine
up.mountOption: Mount repository first (required for forked repos on first deploy)
up.skipCheckpointOption: Skip CRIU checkpoint restore even if checkpoint data exists (force fresh start)
up.grandOption: Parent credential repository (auto-resolves name to GUID). Only for repos sharing secrets with a parent
up.tlsOption: Request dedicated TLS cert for this repo (forks use shared machine cert by default)
up.checkpointOption: Restore CRIU checkpoint on start (resume processes from saved memory state)

upAll.description: Deploy all repositories on a machine
upAll.dryRunOption: Show what would be done without executing

down.description: Stop repository Docker containers (runs Rediaccfile down via renet compose). Does NOT unmount the encrypted volume -- the repo stays mounted and can be restarted with 'repo up'. Use --unmount to also close the LUKS container after stopping. Use --checkpoint to save CRIU process state before stopping (next 'repo up' auto-restores). Omit name to stop all repos on the machine
down.unmountOption: Also unmount (close LUKS container) after stopping. Equivalent to 'repo down' then 'repo unmount'. Required before 'repo resize' or to fully secure the volume
down.checkpointOption: Create CRIU checkpoint before stopping (save process memory state for later restore via 'repo up')
down.grandOption: Parent credential repository (auto-resolves name to GUID). Only for repos sharing secrets with a parent

status.description: Get repository status (mount state, Docker daemon running, container count, disk usage)

create.noDockerOption: Skip starting Docker daemon after creation

delete.description: Delete a repository (destroys containers, volumes, and encrypted image). Config entry is preserved. Use --archive-config to move credentials to deletedRepositories for recovery via 'config restore-archived'
delete.descriptionShort: Delete a repository from a machine
delete.archiveOption: Move config entry to deletedRepositories for later recovery
delete.configRetained: Config entry for "{{repository}}" retained (repo may exist on other machines). To remove: rdc config repository remove {{repository}}
delete.archiveHint: To archive credentials for recovery: rerun with --archive-config

fork.description: Create a CoW (Copy-on-Write) fork of a repository. Fork gets a NEW GUID, networkId, IP range, and auto-route domain ({service}.{forkName}.{machine}.{baseDomain}) — it is a fully independent copy. Online forking is supported — the parent can remain running. Fork inherits the parent's encryption credentials automatically. Use --checkpoint to capture CRIU process state before forking — the fork will auto-restore on first 'repo up' (in-memory state preserved). CROSS-MACHINE FORK: To fork to another machine, first fork locally, then transfer: (1) repo fork <parent> -m <source> --tag <name>, (2) backup push <name> -m <source> --to-machine <target>, (3) repo up <name> -m <target> --mount. WARNING: Do NOT use 'backup push' alone for forking — it creates a raw copy with the SAME GUID (not an independent fork). Always fork first to get a new identity. Auto-routes use the repo name so each fork gets a unique domain automatically
fork.descriptionShort: Create a CoW fork of a repository (new GUID, networkId, IP range)
fork.checkpointOption: Create CRIU checkpoint on source before forking (capture process memory state for restore on fork)
fork.tagRequired: Tag is required for fork. Usage: rdc repo fork <parent> <tag> -m <machine>
fork.completed: Fork created. Deploy locally: repo up {{repository}} -m {{machine}} --mount. To migrate fork to another machine: backup push {{repository}} -m {{machine}} --to-machine <target> && repo up {{repository}} -m <target> --mount

takeover.description: Replace grand repo's data with a fork's data. The grand keeps its identity (GUID, networkId, domains, autostart, backup chain) but gets the fork's upgraded data. The old production data is preserved as a backup fork. Use for: test upgrade on fork → verify → takeover to production.
takeover.descriptionShort: Replace production data with fork data
takeover.notAFork: "{{name}}" is not a fork repository (missing parentGuid)
takeover.grandNotFound: Grand repo not found for fork "{{name}}"
takeover.starting: Taking over "{{grand}}" with data from "{{fork}}" on "{{machine}}"...
takeover.completed: Takeover complete. Grand repo now has fork's data.

resize.description: Resize a repository offline (supports both grow and shrink). Repo must be unmounted first ('repo down --unmount'). For zero-downtime growth without stopping, use 'repo expand' instead
resize.descriptionShort: Resize a repository offline (grow or shrink)

expand.description: Expand a mounted repository online (zero downtime, grow-only). Grows the LUKS container and filesystem while containers keep running. Cannot shrink -- use 'repo resize' for that (requires unmount)
expand.descriptionShort: Expand a mounted repository online (zero downtime, grow-only)

validate.description: Validate repository integrity (LUKS container, filesystem consistency, configuration). Use after unexpected shutdowns or to verify backup health

autostart.description: Manage automatic repository startup on boot

enable.description: Enable autostart for a repository (omit name to enable all)
enable.startingAll: Enabling autostart for all repositories on "{{machine}}"...
enable.failedAll: Failed to enable autostart for all repositories

disable.description: Disable autostart for a repository (omit name to disable all)
disable.startingAll: Disabling autostart for all repositories on "{{machine}}"...
disable.failedAll: Failed to disable autostart for all repositories

ownership.description: Change repository directory ownership UID on the mounted volume (default: 7111). Use when containers need a specific UID to access repo files

template.description: Apply a template to a repository (writes docker-compose.yml and Rediaccfile). Rediaccfile must use 'renet compose' (not 'docker compose'). Restart policies are safe (auto-managed by renet watchdog). Renet auto-injects network_mode:host, CRIU capabilities, and rediacc labels. Dangerous settings (privileged, pid:host) blocked unless --unsafe
template.descriptionShort: Apply a template to a repository (docker-compose.yml + Rediaccfile)
template.fileOption: Path to template JSON file ({"version":"1","compose":"<yaml>","rediaccfile":"<bash>"}). Rediaccfile lifecycle: up() starts containers (pull images, generate configs here), down() stops. Minimal Rediaccfile: up() { renet compose -- pull; renet compose -- up -d; } down() { renet compose -- down; }. IMPORTANT: Rediaccfile MUST use 'renet compose' — 'docker compose' is rejected. ENV VARS — two levels: (a) Rediaccfile shell: ${SVCNAME_IP} (e.g. OPENCLAW_IP), ${REPOSITORY_PATH}, ${REPOSITORY_NETWORK_ID}. (b) Inside containers: renet auto-injects SERVICE_IP and REPOSITORY_NETWORK_ID env vars. Use SERVICE_IP in your app code to bind to the correct loopback IP. Containers MUST bind to SERVICE_IP:<port> (not 0.0.0.0) since network_mode:host is injected and ports: are ignored. STORAGE: Both ${REPOSITORY_PATH}/... bind mounts and Docker named volumes are safe — Docker data-root is inside the encrypted LUKS mount. RESTART POLICY: Restart policies are safe — renet auto-strips them for CRIU compatibility and the watchdog handles recovery. Compose: do NOT add network_mode or rediacc.* labels (renet injects them). The compose service name becomes the auto-route URL prefix (e.g. service 'openclaw' in repo 'myapp' → openclaw.myapp.{machineName}.{baseDomain}). HTTPS routing: (A) Auto-route (fork-friendly, recommended): do NOT add traefik.enable. Renet auto-generates https://{serviceName}.{repoName}.{machineName}.{baseDomain}. Add rediacc.service_port=<port> label for non-80 ports. Each fork gets a unique domain. (B) Traefik labels (custom domain, NOT fork-friendly): traefik.enable=true, traefik.http.routers.<n>.rule=Host(`domain`), traefik.http.routers.<n>.entrypoints=websecure,websecure-v6, traefik.http.routers.<n>.tls.certresolver=letsencrypt, traefik.http.services.<n>.loadbalancer.server.port=<port>. For TCP/UDP: rediacc.tcp_ports=3306 / rediacc.udp_ports=53
template.grandOption: Parent credential repository (auto-resolves name to GUID). Only for repos sharing secrets with a parent

push.description: Push repository backup to storage or machine. Omit name to push all repos. For machine-to-machine transfer (--to-machine), the encrypted repo image is copied directly with the SAME GUID — this is a backup/migration, not a fork. To create an independent fork on another machine, use 'repo fork' first (gets new GUID/networkId), then push the fork. After push, deploy on the target with: repo up <name> -m <target> --mount
push.descriptionShort: Push repository backup to storage or machine
push.optionProvider: Cloud provider to auto-provision target machine if it doesn't exist (from config provider add)
push.optionCheckpoint: Create CRIU container checkpoint before backup (captures process memory state). For live migration: repo push <repo> -m <source> --to-machine <target> --checkpoint, then: repo up <repo> -m <target> --mount --checkpoint (restores process state on target)
push.destIgnoredForStorage: Ignoring --dest {{provided}}: storage backups use repository GUID ({{dest}})
push.optionUp: After push, mount and deploy repository on target machine

pull.description: Pull repository backup from storage or another machine (restores the encrypted LUKS image). Omit name to pull all repos. After pull, deploy with 'repo up <name> -m <machine> --mount'. Use --force to overwrite existing
pull.descriptionShort: Pull repository backup from storage or machine

list.description: List available backups on storage or machine

sync.description: Sync repositories and files (bulk push/pull, file transfer)

push.description: Push repositories to cloud storage (omit name to push all)

pull.description: Pull repositories from cloud storage (omit name to pull all)
pull.optionOverride: Override existing unmounted repos on pull

upload.description: Upload files to a repository via rsync over SSH (delta transfer). Use --mirror to delete extra remote files, --dry-run to preview, --exclude to skip patterns
upload.descriptionShort: Upload files to a repository via rsync

download.description: Download files from a repository via rsync over SSH (delta transfer). Use --mirror to delete extra local files, --dry-run to preview, --exclude to skip patterns
download.descriptionShort: Download files from a repository via rsync

status.description: Dry-run comparison of local and remote files (shows what would be transferred without actually transferring)

snapshot.description: Manage BTRFS snapshots on remote machines

create.description: Create a point-in-time BTRFS snapshot of a repository (instant, no downtime). Use --snapshot-name for a custom name
create.descriptionShort: Create a BTRFS snapshot of a repository

list.description: List BTRFS snapshots on a remote machine with name, creation date, and size

delete.description: Delete a snapshot from a remote machine

backup.description: Push, pull, and sync repository backups across storage and machines

sync.description: Bidirectional sync of repository backups across machines

schedule.description: Deploy backup schedules to remote machines

storage.descriptionShort: Manage storage providers and browse remote files
storage.description: Manage remote backup storage providers (S3, rclone-compatible). CRUD operations (list, create, rename, delete), vault configuration, browse remote files, and prune orphaned backups. This is for backup storage — not to be confused with `store` which syncs CLI config files.

browse.description: Browse files in a storage system

prune.description: Delete orphaned backups from storage that are no longer in any config. Multi-config safe with grace period protection.

pull.description: Pull a backup from cloud storage to a machine
pull.noCredential: Warning: Repository "{{name}}" has no credential set. Pull may fail if the backup is encrypted.

sync.description: Transfer files between local and remote repositories via rsync
sync.dryRunHeader: Dry run - showing what would be transferred:

upload.description: Upload files to a repository via rsync over SSH (delta transfer). Use --mirror to delete extra remote files, --dry-run to preview, --exclude to skip patterns
upload.descriptionShort: Upload files to a repository via rsync

download.description: Download files from a repository via rsync over SSH (delta transfer). Use --mirror to delete extra local files, --dry-run to preview, --exclude to skip patterns
download.descriptionShort: Download files from a repository via rsync

status.description: Dry-run comparison of local and remote files (shows what would be transferred without actually transferring)

subscription.description: Manage machine activation, licensing, and subscription tokens. Key subcommands: login (authenticate with subscription portal), status (show current license state), activation (activate/deactivate machines), repo (manage per-repo licenses).

login.description: Authenticate via browser or API token
login.tokenPrompt: Enter your API token (from web portal):
login.polling: Waiting for approval (press Ctrl+C to cancel)...
login.expired: Authorization timed out. Please try again.
login.browserFailed: Could not open browser automatically. Please visit the URL above.
login.initFailed: Failed to initiate login. Is the server running?

status.description: Show subscription, machine activation, and repo license status
status.expired: Activation needs refresh — run `rdc subscription refresh -m <machine>`
status.parseFailed: Could not read remote machine status
status.serverMismatch: Dev token belongs to {{actualServerUrl}}, but this worktree is using {{expectedServerUrl}}. Run `rdc subscription login` again.
status.remoteRepoLicenses: Repo licenses: {{totalTrackedRepos}} tracked, {{validCount}} valid, {{refreshRecommendedCount}} refresh recommended, {{hardExpiredCount}} hard expired
status.issuanceLimitReached: Monthly repo license issuance limit reached for the current UTC calendar month.
status.issuanceUsageHigh95: Monthly repo license issuances are above 95% of the current UTC limit.
status.issuanceUsageHigh80: Monthly repo license issuances are above 80% of the current UTC limit.

status.description: Show machine activation status for one machine
status.unavailable: Activation: could not query account state
status.inactive: Activation: machine is not currently active in account state

status.description: Show installed repo licenses on a machine

refresh.description: Refresh machine activation and repo licenses on a remote machine
refresh.success: Machine activation refreshed and repo licenses processed

activation.description: Refresh machine activation on a remote machine

repos.description: Batch-refresh repo licenses on a remote machine

repo.description: Refresh the repo license for a specific repository

add.description: Add a user to a team

remove.description: Remove a user from a team

run.description: WARNING: Low-level command for debugging and experimentation only. Not for production use. Use higher-level commands (repo up, repo down, etc.) instead. Runs a raw bridge function on a machine.
run.descriptionShort: Run a raw bridge function on a machine (debugging only)

trace.description: Trace a task (shortcut for: queue trace)

cancel.description: Cancel a task (shortcut for: queue cancel)

retry.description: Retry a failed task (shortcut for: queue retry)

update.descriptionShort: Check for and apply CLI updates
update.description: Check for new CLI versions and apply updates. Supports --check-only to check without installing, and --force to update even if already on the latest version.
update.force: Force update even if already up-to-date
update.checkOnly: Only check for updates without downloading
update.notSEA: Cannot update: not running as a packaged binary. Build with SEA first.
update.autoApplied: Updated to v{{version}} (was v{{from}}). Changes take effect on next run.
update.staged: Update v{{version}} staged (will apply on next launch)
update.stagedFallback: Update v{{version}} downloaded and staged. It will be applied on next launch.
update.rollbackSuccess: Rolled back successfully. Restart rdc to use the previous version.
update.rollbackNoBackup: No previous version found. Rollback is only available after an update.

term.descriptionShort: SSH terminal access to machines and repositories
term.description: Open an SSH terminal to a machine or repository context. Sets up DOCKER_HOST, working directory, and environment automatically. Supports container actions via --container: logs, exec, stats, terminal. Use `-c` to run a single command non-interactively. For VS Code, use `vscode` instead.

connect.description: Connect to a machine or repository via SSH

vscode.descriptionShort: Open VS Code Remote SSH to a machine or repository
vscode.description: Open VS Code with Remote SSH to a machine or repository with sandboxed per-repo environment. Same SSH connection as `term` but launches VS Code instead. Key subcommands: connect (or positional shorthand), list (show configured connections), cleanup (remove SSH config entries), check (verify VS Code installation).

connect.description: Connect to a machine or repository in VS Code
connect.extensionWarning:
Warning: Remote SSH extension may not be installed.
Install from: https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=ms-vscode-remote.remote-ssh
connect.settingsWarning: Warning: Could not configure VS Code settings: {{error}}

list.description: List configured VS Code SSH connections
list.noConnections: No VS Code SSH connections configured.

cleanup.cleanedAll: Cleaned up {{count}} VS Code SSH connection(s).

check.description: Check VS Code installation and configuration
check.title: VS Code Setup Check

assign.description: Assign a permission group to a user

snapshot.description: Manage BTRFS snapshots on remote machines

help.keyConcepts:
Key Concepts:

  Architecture
    • Each repository = isolated Docker environment (own daemon, loopback IP range, encrypted LUKS storage)
    • Use renet compose (never raw docker compose) — auto-injects network_mode:host and rediacc.* labels
    • Storage: bind-mount to ${REPOSITORY_PATH}/... — never Docker named volumes (excluded from forks/backups)
    • Containers must bind to SERVICE_IP to avoid port conflicts

  Routing
    • Auto-route (fork-friendly): https://{service}.{repo}.{machine}.{baseDomain}
    • Custom domain: Traefik labels (traefik.enable=true) — not fork-friendly
    • rediacc.service_port label for non-80 ports. Routes activate ~3s after deploy
    • HTTPS: automatic Let's Encrypt certs via Cloudflare DNS-01. Grand repos get dedicated wildcard certs
    • Forks share the machine wildcard cert by default (zero cert consumption). Use --tls on repo up for dedicated cert

  Environment Variables
    • Rediaccfile shell: ${SVCNAME_IP}, ${REPOSITORY_PATH}, ${REPOSITORY_NETWORK_ID}
    • Inside containers: SERVICE_IP, REPOSITORY_NETWORK_ID (auto-injected by renet)

  Forking
    • repo fork = instant CoW clone with new GUID, networkId, IP range, auto-route domain
    • Parent stays running (online forking). Cross-machine: fork locally, then backup push
    • Config entry persists after repo delete (repo may exist on other machines)
help.agentMode:
Agent Mode
    • Fork-only: create a fork before making changes to any repository
    • Grand (non-fork) repositories are read-only for agents
    • Workflow: repo fork <parent> -m <machine> → repo up <fork> -m <machine> --mount → term <machine> <fork>

machine.keyConcepts:
Key Concepts:

  Query Filters
    • rdc machine query <name> returns all sections by default
    • Use --system, --repositories, --containers, --services, --network, --block-devices to filter
    • JSON output enriches containers with: repository (resolved name), domain, autoRoute

  Health & Monitoring
    • machine health exits with: 0=healthy, 1=warning, 2=error — use for CI/CD gates
    • containers --health-check exits 2 if any container is unhealthy
    • services --stability-check exits 2 if any service is failed, restarting, or has >3 restarts
    • machine prune removes orphaned loopback IPs and systemd units from deleted repos

  Datastore Structure (/mnt/rediacc/)
    • repositories/ — encrypted LUKS images (one per repo GUID)
    • mounts/ — decrypted mount points (accessible when repo is mounted)
    • immovable/ — per-repo Docker daemon data
    • Datastore is a BTRFS pool file on the system disk. Effective free space = min(disk free, datastore free). Expand with: rdc datastore resize

repo.keyConcepts:
Key Concepts:

  Compose & Rediaccfile
    • renet compose -- up -d (never raw docker compose). The -- separator is required
    • Rediaccfile must define up() and down() functions. up() should: create dirs, generate configs, start containers
    • compose ports: are ignored (network_mode:host). Use rediacc.service_port label for external access
    • restart: always/unless-stopped are auto-stripped by renet and saved to .rediacc.json — the router watchdog auto-recovers stopped services
    • Each service gets a loopback IP. Bind to ${SERVICE_IP} in commands/configs
    • Health checks must use ${SERVICE_IP}, not localhost. Example: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://${SERVICE_IP}:8080/health"]

  Storage
    • Bind-mount to ${REPOSITORY_PATH}/data, ${REPOSITORY_PATH}/config, etc.
    • Example: volumes: ['${REPOSITORY_PATH}/data:/var/lib/postgresql/data']
    • Docker named volumes are excluded from forks and backups
    • Config files bind-mounted into containers must be world-readable (644) if the container runs as a non-default UID

  Routing
    • Grand repo auto-route: https://{service}.{repo}.{machine}.{baseDomain} (dedicated wildcard cert)
    • Fork auto-route: https://{service}-{tag}.{machine}.{baseDomain} (shared machine cert, zero rate limit impact)
    • Custom domain: traefik.enable=true + traefik.http.routers.{name}.rule=Host(`domain.com`)
    • Use --tls on repo up to request a dedicated cert for a fork (50 certs/week limit per domain)
    • TCP/UDP forwarding: rediacc.tcp_ports=5432 / rediacc.udp_ports=53
    • Custom domains (rediacc.domain label) are ignored on forks — the domain belongs to the grand repo

  Lifecycle
    • repo delete fully cleans up: storage, Docker daemon, loopback IPs, systemd units
    • repo down stops containers but keeps Docker daemon and IPs (quick restart with repo up)
    • depends_on with condition: service_healthy handles startup order but not runtime recovery
    • If a database stops cleanly (exit 0), dependent services crash-loop. Fix: rdc repo up -m <machine>
    • Autostart: rdc repo autostart enable <name> -m <machine> — auto-mounts and starts repo on machine boot
    • Scheduled backups skip fork repos automatically. Use rdc repo push for manual fork backup
    • repo takeover <fork> -m <machine> — replace production data with fork data (preserves identity). Revert with: repo takeover <backup-fork>
repo.takeover: Replace production data with fork data

sync.pushAll: Bulk push all repos to storage
sync.pullAll: Bulk pull all repos from storage

config.init: Create named config with SSH key

errors.authRequired: Authentication required. Run: rdc auth login
errors.bridgeRequired: Bridge name required. Use --bridge or set default with: rdc config set bridge <name>
errors.invalidMasterPassword: Invalid master password for vault encryption
errors.invalidExpiration: Token expiration must be between 1 and 720 hours
errors.invalidJsonFile: The provided file does not contain valid JSON.
errors.machineRequired: Machine name required. Use --machine or set default with: rdc config set machine <name>
errors.machineRequiredLocal: Machine name required. Use --machine or set default with: rdc config set machine <name>
errors.invalidHost: Invalid IP address or hostname: "{{host}}". Must be a valid IPv4, IPv6, or hostname.
errors.invalidPort: Invalid port number: "{{port}}". Must be between 1 and 65535.
errors.invalidPriorityMin: Invalid priority-min value. Must be between 1 and 5.
errors.invalidPriorityMax: Invalid priority-max value. Must be between 1 and 5.
errors.affectedChildClones: . Affected child clones: {{clones}}. Delete or promote child clones first.

protocol.notSupported: Protocol registration is not supported on {{platform}}
protocol.invalidAction: Invalid action '{{action}}'. Must be one of: {{validActions}}

errors.noActiveConfig: No active config. Create one with: rdc config init <name>
errors.notAuthenticated: Not authenticated. Run: rdc auth login
errors.regionRequired: Region name required. Use --region or set default with: rdc config set region <name>

ssh.noIpAddress: Machine '{{machine}}' does not have an IP address configured
ssh.noPrivateKey: Team '{{team}}' does not have an SSH private key configured
ssh.noHostKey: Machine '{{machine}}' does not have a host key configured in the vault

sync.noIpAddress: Machine '{{machine}}' does not have an IP address configured
sync.noPrivateKey: Team '{{team}}' does not have an SSH private key configured
sync.noHostKey: Machine '{{machine}}' does not have a host key configured in the vault
sync.absolutePathBlocked: The --remote path must be relative, not absolute. Use a path relative to the repository root.
sync.pathTraversalBlocked: The --remote path must not contain ".." path traversal. Use a path within the repository.

subscription.notLoggedIn: Not logged in. Humans should run `rdc subscription login`. Automation should run `rdc subscription login --token <token>` or set `REDIACC_SUBSCRIPTION_TOKEN`.
subscription.tokenRequired: Subscription token required. Either set REDIACC_SUBSCRIPTION_TOKEN env var, or run `rdc subscription login` in your terminal.
subscription.activationFailed: Machine activation failed. Ensure your subscription is active and the machine slot is available.
subscription.tokenWarning: No subscription token found. License-required commands (repo create, repo fork) will fail. Set REDIACC_SUBSCRIPTION_TOKEN env var or run `rdc subscription login` in your terminal.

errors.teamRequired: Team name required. Use --team or set default with: rdc config set team <name>

term.noHostKey: Machine '{{machine}}' does not have a host key configured in the vault
term.noIpAddress: Machine '{{machine}}' does not have an IP address configured
term.noPrivateKey: Team '{{team}}' does not have an SSH private key configured
term.sshCode1: Generic error - check command syntax or remote permissions
term.sshCode126: Permission denied - cannot execute command on remote
term.sshCode127: Command not found on remote machine
term.sshCode255:
SSH connection failed - possible causes:
    - Authentication failed (check SSH key permissions)
    - Host key verification failed
    - Network connection dropped
    - Remote SSH service not running
term.repoContextRequired:
Command requires repository context.

Detected "{{detected}}" which needs DOCKER_HOST and repository environment variables.
These are only available when a repository is specified.

Instead of:
  rdc term {{machine}} -c "{{command}}"

Use:
  rdc term {{machine}} <repository> -c "{{command}}"

vscode.cleanupRequired: Specify --all to remove all connections or --connection <name> for a specific one.
vscode.noHostKey: Machine '{{machine}}' does not have a host key configured in the vault
vscode.noIpAddress: Machine '{{machine}}' does not have an IP address configured
vscode.noPrivateKey: Team '{{team}}' does not have an SSH private key configured
vscode.notFound:
VS Code not found.

Please install from: https://code.visualstudio.com/
Or set REDIACC_VSCODE_PATH environment variable.
vscode.repoNotDeployed:
Repository "{{repository}}" is not deployed on {{machine}}.

Deploy it first:
  rdc repo up {{repository}} -m <machine-name>

errors.vaultDataRequired: Vault data required. Use --vault <json> or pipe JSON via stdin.

agent.grandGuard:
Cannot modify grand repository "{{name}}" in agent mode.

AI agents can only operate on fork repositories.
Fork it first:
  rdc repo fork {{name}} -m <machine> --tag <tag-name>

Then operate on the fork instead.

To override: set REDIACC_ALLOW_GRAND_REPO={{name}}
agent.mcpGrandGuard:
Cannot modify grand repository "{{name}}" in fork-only mode.

Use repo_fork to create a fork first, then operate on the fork.
To allow grand repository access, restart MCP server with --allow-grand
agent.machineGuard:
Cannot access machine "{{machine}}" directly in agent mode.

AI agents can only connect to repository contexts.
Use:
  rdc term {{machine}} <repository> -c "<command>"

To override: set REDIACC_ALLOW_GRAND_REPO=*
agent.mcpMachineGuard:
Cannot access machine directly in fork-only mode.

Specify a repository to connect to a repository context.
To allow direct machine access, restart MCP server with --allow-grand
agent.forkBlocked:
Command "{{command}}" is not available on fork repositories.

Fork repos are interim environments. This operation only applies to grand repositories.
agent.createGuard:
Cannot create repository "{{name}}" in agent mode.

AI agents operate in fork-only mode. Creating a grand repo is blocked
because the agent cannot operate on it afterwards.

Use an existing repo and fork it instead:
  rdc repo fork <parent> -m <machine> --tag {{name}}

To override: set REDIACC_ALLOW_GRAND_REPO=*

config.storageNotFound: Storage "{{name}}" not found in config. Available: {{available}}.
config.machineNotFound: Machine "{{name}}" not found in config. Available: {{available}}.

options.saveAs: Save credentials to a named config
options.switch: Switch to this config after creation
options.fields: Comma-separated list of fields to include in output

options.newWindow: Open in new VS Code window
options.vaultVersion: Current vault version for optimistic locking
options.vaultJsonMachine: Machine vault data as JSON string
options.machineForVault: Machine name (for updating vault after test)
options.saveKnownHosts: Save known_hosts to machine vault after successful test
options.mirrorUpload: Mirror mode - delete remote files not present locally
options.mirrorDownload: Mirror mode - delete local files not present on remote
options.verifyChecksum: Verify files using checksums after sync
options.confirmSync: Preview changes and ask for confirmation before syncing
options.dryRun: Show what would be done without making changes
options.graceDays: Grace period in days for recently archived repos (default: 7)
options.searchQueue: Search in task ID, team, machine, bridge
options.protocolForce: Force re-registration even if already registered
options.skipRouterRestart: Skip restarting the route server after binary update

prompts.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this?
prompts.sshPassword: SSH password (leave empty if using key auth):
prompts.syncConfirmHelp: Please enter y (yes), n (no), or d (details)
prompts.masterPasswordForVault: Enter master password for vault encryption:

frontmatter.title: CLI Application
frontmatter.description: Complete guide to using the Rediacc command-line interface for platform management

overview.heading: Overview
overview.text: The **Rediacc CLI** (`rdc`) is a command-line interface for managing self-hosted infrastructure — machines, repositories, storage, backups, and more.

cloudFrontmatter.title: CLI Application (Cloud / Experimental)
cloudFrontmatter.description: Cloud-only commands for the Rediacc CLI — authentication, teams, organizations, and multi-tenant management

cloudOverview.heading: Overview
cloudOverview.text: These commands require the **cloud adapter** (experimental) — activated when a config contains `apiUrl` and `token` fields. For the main CLI reference, see [CLI Application](/en/docs/cli-application).

installation.heading: Installation
installation.text: Download the latest binary for your platform from the [Rediacc releases page](https://www.rediacc.com) or install via your package manager:

globalOptions.heading: Global Options
globalOptions.intro: Every command supports these global flags:

errors.heading: Common Error Messages
errors.intro: When something goes wrong, the CLI provides clear error messages:

meanings.authRequired: You need to log in first
meanings.noActiveConfig: No config is active — create one with `rdc config init`
meanings.permissionDenied: Your account lacks the required permissions
meanings.machineRequired: Specify a machine with `--machine` or set a config default
meanings.teamRequired: Specify a team with `--team` or set a config default
meanings.regionRequired: Specify a region with `--region` or set a config default

outputFormats.heading: Output Formats
outputFormats.text: All list/get commands support multiple output formats via `--output`:
outputFormats.closing: This makes it easy to integrate Rediacc CLI into scripts, CI/CD pipelines, and automation workflows.

auth.tip: Your authentication token is stored locally in your config. Use `--save-as` to store credentials under a named config for multiple environments.

register.afterDescription: After registration, check your email for the activation code.

config.afterDescription: Config files store connection details (API URL, credentials, default team/region/machine) so you can switch between environments quickly.

set.tip: Setting defaults means you don't need to pass `--team`, `--region`, or `--machine` with every command.

maintenance.warning: When maintenance mode is enabled, non-admin users cannot log in.

health.afterDescription: The health command outputs system metrics, container status, service stability, and storage information — useful for CI/CD pipeline checks.

testConnection.tip: Use `--save-known-hosts` to automatically persist the host key to the machine vault after a successful test.

promote.note: Promoting a fork separates it from its grand (parent) repository, making it independent.

queue.afterDescription: The queue system is at the heart of Rediacc's task execution. Every operation (deploy, backup, script execution) runs through the queue.

trace.tip: Use `--watch` to follow the task in real-time until it completes.

prune.refusingPrune: Refusing to prune — these configs may reference protected GUIDs. Use --force to override.
